resolvedFindings.finding: Supported agent list was incomplete or GJC-only in prominent copy.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Every README now names Gajae Code (GJC), Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and OpenCode.

resolvedFindings.finding: Electron development commands appeared sequential even though the first process remains active.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Every README now separates npm run dev and npm run desktop:dev into Terminal 1 and Terminal 2.

resolvedFindings.finding: The root README delegated core installation details instead of being self-contained.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Every README now includes development installation and the immutable, checksum-verified Node 22 production artifact and user-systemd first-install flow.

resolvedFindings.finding: package.json described a Claude-only web UI.
resolvedFindings.resolution: The package description now identifies the self-hosted web and desktop multi-agent product.

resolvedFindings.finding: Localized READMEs had stale provider, logo, installation, verification, security, and license guidance.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Seven localized READMEs now mirror the canonical section, command, link, security, CI, release, and GNU AGPL v3 contracts.

consistencyChecks.packageDescription: A self-hosted web and desktop interface for GJC, Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and OpenCode

verification.identity: passed: 779 source, 579 generated, 1 archive file scanned

correctiveVerification: The first verification attempt exposed missing exact upstream-lineage blocks in localized READMEs.
The second focused identity check exposed that the canonical root README must omit the legacy upstream coordinate while localized READMEs must retain the scanner's exact protected lineage block.
Both identity-contract defects were corrected before the final successful npm run verify.

delivery.authorization: Hako granted autonomous Git commit and push authority for completed, verified work.
